# Infrastructure

<!-- Generated by /init — defines deployment and infrastructure plans -->

## Deployment Target

- **App:** distributed through the **Apple App Store**, with **TestFlight** for
  beta builds. iPhone, iOS 16+. Requires an **Apple Developer Program membership
  ($99/yr)** — to be acquired (see `CONSTRAINTS.md`).
- **No backend of our own / no accounts.** We host only static files (the dataset
  + manifest). Scans are answered on-device by default; the **opt-in online
  fallback** (off by default) calls third-party lookup APIs (e.g. the free OFF API)
  **directly from the app** — still no server of ours, and no user data stored
  anywhere (see `STACK.md` privacy notes).

## CI/CD

Two separate pipelines:

- **Data pipeline — GitHub Actions (daily).** A scheduled workflow (`on: schedule`,
  daily cron) downloads the Open Food Facts dump, filters it to target-company
  barcodes, joins the curated brand→parent table, rebuilds the SQLite, bumps the
  version manifest, and **publishes both to GitHub Releases**. Runs on a Linux
  runner. This is what delivers the daily dataset refresh.
- **App build/release — Xcode Cloud.** Builds, tests, signs, and ships the app to
  TestFlight / App Store with native App Store Connect integration. (Free tier:
  25 compute hrs/mo.)

## Database Hosting

- **On-device (primary):** the SQLite dataset lives on the device. v1 ships with a
  bundled copy inside the app, so it works fully offline from first launch.
- **Dataset distribution (for self-update): GitHub Releases.** The latest SQLite +
  a small JSON **version manifest** are published as release assets. The app checks
  the manifest **daily**; if the version bumped, it downloads the new SQLite and
  swaps it in locally. This is a plain static file `GET` — no backend, no database
  server, no telemetry.
- **ODbL publication:** GitHub Releases doubles as the public download point for
  the derived dataset, satisfying the ODbL share-alike obligation (see
  `CONSTRAINTS.md`).
- **Refresh cadence: daily.**

## Domain & DNS

- **GitHub Pages** hosts the required **privacy policy** page — **live** at
  <https://tonylchang.github.io/isNestle/> (served from `/docs` on `main`, linked
  from the app's Settings). The repo is now **public** (matching the MIT /
  open-source plan), which also unlocks free Actions and serves as the ODbL
  publication point.
- **Custom domain: TBD.** The default `*.github.io` URL is fine to start; a custom
  domain (e.g., `isnestle.app`) can be added later.

## Monitoring & Observability

- **None — by design.** Consistent with the strict privacy stance, there is no
  analytics, no remote crash reporting, and no telemetry that leaves the device.
  There is no server to monitor.
- App stability is observed only through Apple's built-in, user-consented App Store
  crash diagnostics (opt-in at the OS level, not an in-app SDK) and TestFlight
  feedback during beta.
